ground.what_this_is: This is the Scenario Player interface, a warm, gold-themed control room where you can step through a live simulation of the system, switch between different 'personas' (Vi, Atlas, Nova), and toggle between phone and desktop views to see how the system behaves.
ground.what_you_can_do: Click the state buttons at the top to advance or rewind the simulation, tap the persona cards to change the system's identity and color scheme, and use the frame toggle to switch between mobile and desktop layouts.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is ScenarioPlayer.tsx; the voice wire is RHM-verbs (the twin's listening channel).
howto.what: The push-to-talk control on the Scenario Player interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate a voice command to the system's twin, allowing the operator to speak directly to the live simulation state.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the action logic is driven by suite.py:chat (specifically the RHM-verbs wire).
howto.what: The 'reply' command button on the Scenario Player control panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to trigger a reply action from the live system state, allowing the operator to advance the simulation's narrative flow.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific button yet.
howto.what: The consent control on the Scenario Player interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to grant or deny the system's permission to proceed with the current simulation step.

dossier.represents: The consent checkpoint in the Scenario Player

howto.how_to_change: The surface is ScenarioPlayer.tsx; the view logic is driven by the simulation state machine.
howto.what: The phone view switch on the Scenario Player control room.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to switch the simulation's layout from desktop to mobile (phone) view, allowing you to see how the system behaves on a phone screen.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is ScenarioPlayer.tsx; the simulation logic and persona states live in the runtime simulation module.
howto.what: The composition canvas — the Scenario Player's control room where you step through a live simulation of the system.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click state buttons to advance or rewind the simulation, tap persona cards to change the system's identity and color scheme, and use the frame toggle to switch between mobile and desktop layouts.

howto.how_to_change: The text is a static display in the Scenario Player interface; changing it requires editing the Scenario Player's display logic, not the runtime code.
howto.what: A system node explaining its caching behavior versus volatile re-runs.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the explanation of how the system distinguishes between cached results and nodes that bypass the gate to always re-run.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the approval logic is driven by suite.py:chat (specifically the RHM-decide wire).
howto.what: The approval trigger on the Scenario Player interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to formally approve an action proposed by the RHM (Right-Hand-Man) system, recording your verdict and allowing the simulation to proceed.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the logic for this specific verb is in suite.py:chat.
howto.what: The 'not now' control on the chat interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to pause or defer the current system response or action without resolving it immediately.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only surface; the real state generation logic lives in the parent region (RhmChat.tsx) and the system state in suite.py:chat. Changing the text requires a code update to the parent region.
howto.what: The empty state message displayed when the RHM-chat has no active conversation or system state to present.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the message to understand that the chat is currently idle; no interaction is possible until state is generated.

dossier.represents: The empty state of the RHM-chat

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the query handling is driven by the RHM-chat brain in suite.py:chat.
howto.what: The input field in the Scenario Player control room where you ask the Company about itself.
howto.what_you_can_do: Type a question to the system (it answers from live state and says so when it can't see something); this is the primary interface for querying the RHM twin.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is ScenarioPlayer.tsx; the trigger logic is driven by the simulation state machine (mockup-only; no built code path maps to it yet).
howto.what: The 'release to send' button on the Scenario Player interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Advance the live simulation of the system by triggering the next state change or action in the scenario.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the identity logic is RHM-config or RHM-modes.
howto.what: The system's nameplate in the Scenario Player control room.
howto.what_you_can_do: Identify the current persona (Vi, Atlas, or Nova) and the system's operational state at a glance.

reasons: refcheck (Layer 1, deterministic floor): feature fabrication: maps_to_feature 'RHM-identity' is not an inventory id nor 'proposed'
accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (0 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.
